skill.name: threejs-animation
skill.description: Guidance for creating Three.js animations with keyframes, mixers, skeletal rigs, morph targets, blending, and procedural motion patterns.
skill.summary: Build and tune Three.js animation workflows for objects, characters, morph targets, and GLTF clips.
skill.author: cloudai-x

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged many Markdown code fences as Ruby or shell backtick execution, but the file contains documentation examples for Three.js. The weak cryptography and certificate key alerts are false positives from ordinary words and Object.keys usage, with no prompt injection, credential access, installer script, or data exfiltration evidence found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences Flagged as External Commands
low_findings.description: Static analysis treated JavaScript Markdown fences as Ruby or shell backtick execution. These locations are documentation snippets that explain Three.js animation APIs and are not executable skill scripts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Each flagged location is a Markdown code fence or JavaScript example in SKILL.md. No shell command, Ruby backtick execution, or runtime wrapper is present in the scanned file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Matches Documentation Text
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm alerts occur in descriptive Three.js animation text, including the front matter description, interpolation mode heading, AnimationMixer description, and loop mode comment. No cryptographic API or hashing behavior is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines discuss Three.js animation concepts, not encryption or hashing. I did not find calls to weak algorithms or any credential protection logic.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Object.keys Flagged as Certificate or Key File Access
low_findings.description: The sensitive-file alert points to Object.keys used to list morph target attributes. This is ordinary JavaScript object introspection and does not read certificate files, private keys, or filesystem paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 320 logs keys from geometry.morphAttributes inside a Three.js example. There is no filesystem access, secret filename, or certificate material in the surrounding context.

content.user_title: Animate Three.js Scenes With Guided Patterns
content.value_statement: Three.js animation work can become difficult when keyframes, mixers, rigs, and morph targets interact. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code focused guidance for building reliable animation flows.
actual_capabilities: Explains how to create AnimationClip and KeyframeTrack objects for positions, rotations, colors, and visibility.
Shows how AnimationMixer and AnimationAction control clip playback, looping, fading, and crossfading.
Guides loading and playing GLTF animation clips with named clip selection.
Covers skeletal animation tasks such as bone lookup, bone rotation, helpers, and attachments.
Documents morph target influence control and keyframed morph animation.
Provides procedural motion patterns, including smoothing, springs, oscillation, and animation performance tips.
limitations: It is a documentation skill and does not include runnable project files or installed dependencies.
It assumes the user already has a Three.js scene, renderer, camera, and animation loop.
It does not provide complete character rigs, model assets, or exported GLTF files.
It does not replace profiling on target hardware for complex animation scenes.

use_cases.title: Prototype object motion
use_cases.description: Create smooth position, rotation, scale, and color animation patterns for interactive WebGL scenes.

use_cases.title: Integrate animated GLTF models
use_cases.description: Load a model, inspect available clips, play named animations, and update mixers correctly in the render loop.

use_cases.title: Blend character actions
use_cases.description: Plan idle, walk, run, additive, skeletal, and morph target animation behavior for game-like interfaces.

prompt_templates.title: Start a basic animation loop
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for simple procedural motion.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the threejs-animation skill to add simple procedural motion to my existing Three.js mesh. Include clock usage, delta time, and render-loop placement.

prompt_templates.title: Build keyframed object motion
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate keyframe animation for a scene object.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the threejs-animation skill to create a keyframed animation for position, rotation, and material color. Explain which KeyframeTrack types I should use.

prompt_templates.title: Play and blend GLTF clips
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced model animation with mixer actions.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the threejs-animation skill to load a GLTF model, list its animation clips, play a named clip, and crossfade to another clip.

prompt_templates.title: Design a character animation system
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced character behavior with blending and optimization.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the threejs-animation skill to design an idle, walk, run, additive, bone attachment, and morph target animation plan for a Three.js character.

output_examples.input: Animate a floating object in my scene.
output_examples.output: A concise plan that adds clock-based vertical motion, keeps movement frame-rate independent, and updates the renderer inside the animation loop.

output_examples.input: Blend walk and run clips from a GLTF character.
output_examples.output: A step-by-step explanation for creating mixer actions, setting weights from speed, crossfading between actions, and updating the mixer each frame.

output_examples.input: Add facial expression changes with morph targets.
output_examples.output: Guidance for locating morph target indices, setting influence values, and creating a short keyframed expression animation.

best_practices: Use delta time from THREE.Clock so animation speed stays stable across different frame rates.
Keep mixer.update calls in the main render loop and pause updates when animated objects are not visible.
Name clips, bones, and morph targets clearly so lookup and blending logic stays easy to maintain.
anti_patterns: Do not update animations with fixed frame assumptions when the render rate can vary.
Do not create many independent mixers for repeated models when shared clips and careful reuse are possible.
Do not blend actions without resetting weights, fade state, and loop behavior for each transition.

faq.question: Does this skill generate a complete Three.js project?
faq.answer: No. It provides focused animation guidance and examples for projects that already use Three.js.

faq.question: Can it help with GLTF character animations?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains loading clips, creating mixers, playing named actions, and blending between animations.

faq.question: Does it cover skeletal animation?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes bone lookup, bone rotation, skeleton helpers, and attaching objects to bones.

faq.question: Does it cover morph targets?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains morph target dictionaries, influence values, and keyframed morph target changes.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The static alerts were false positives in Markdown documentation, and no executable threat was found.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
